danger_signals.visual_form: Yellow rectangle surrounding selected enemy units
danger_signals.meaning: Identifies hostile targets in combat; signals threat proximity and targeting status

danger_signals.visual_form: Diminishing health bar beside unit yellow selection rectangle
danger_signals.meaning: Shows unit vulnerability and damage taken; critical when depleted indicates unit death imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct unit models belonging to Dark army (Balor's forces)
danger_signals.meaning: Identifies enemy faction in multiplayer mode; visual differentiation from Light units

danger_signals.visual_form: Map areas under Dark control vs. Light control
danger_signals.meaning: Communicates strategic danger—almost entire land dominated by Dark; only one major city and few towns remain Light-controlled

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct unit models for Light army forces (Avatara sorcerers, The Legion)
safety_signals.meaning: Identifies friendly units and controlled forces; signals player-controllable army in single-player

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual representation of Light-held cities and towns on battlefield map
safety_signals.meaning: Indicates safe zones and strategic holdings; shows player faction strength and defensive positions

safety_signals.visual_form: Yellow rectangle surrounding selected friendly units with adjacent health meter
safety_signals.meaning: Clear identification of controlled units and their status; enables tactical positioning

safety_signals.visual_form: New units appearing at predetermined locations during missions
safety_signals.meaning: Signals tactical advantage and resource replenishment; shows mission progression support

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: The Nine sorcerers / Alric as chief avatar
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct unit models visually differentiated from Dark forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: One major city and few smaller towns held by Light forces; limited territorial control in a land mostly dominated by Dark

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Balor as supreme leader; group of Fallen Lords as lieutenants
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct unit models visually differentiated from Light forces; playable as opposing faction in multiplayer
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Controls almost entire land; dominant territorial presence; 50+ years since Balor's rise indicates established dominion

world_state_to_visual.world_state: War in stalemate - Light on brink of defeat
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited Light-held territories (one major city, few towns) vs. vast Dark-controlled areas across named mythical land
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Light forces consolidated into defensive positions; Dark forces in dominant offensive posture; The Legion functions as counter-offensive elite unit attempting to turn tide

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Established Dark faction infrastructure and unit presence throughout environment; Light faction fragmented into isolated strongholds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Light forces fight desperately to regain territory; missions begin with pre-assembled armies (no resource gathering needed), suggesting survival-focused tactical gameplay rather than economy building

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Seventeenth year of war in the West
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ongoing conflict visible in battlefield states, unit compositions, and territorial control markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players receive reinforcements at predetermined mission points, indicating strategic attrition and logistical support systems within Light faction

environmental_storytelling.example: Visual representation of which faction controls regions communicates strategic balance; Light's limited holdings (one city, few towns) vs. Dark's dominance tells story of long war grinding Light into desperate corner
Specific unit types (Berserks, Archers, Dwarves) available at mission start communicate tactical options and implied historical/cultural faction differences without explicit exposition

Predetermined reinforcement points communicate narrative of Light forces receiving support from surviving allied regions; absence of reinforcements in late missions would signal escalating desperation
Single-player campaign progression through different battlefields tells story of Light's counter-offensive attempts; map control changes across missions communicate narrative momentum

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Respond to click commands to move to designated ground locations or attack enemy targets; obey grouped selection via keyboard shortcuts indicating disciplined military unit behavior
Same command-response system as Light units; visual distinction allows player identification of enemy faction allegiance
Can be ordered to attack specific ground locations or enemies; ranged attack behavior differentiates them from melee units (Berserks)
Melee-focused unit type; yellow selection rectangles group multiple Berserks together indicating squad-level organization
Health meter visibly diminishes with damage taken; no regeneration means persistent damage communication; visual depletion signals imminent unit loss

notes: Myth: The Fallen Lords uses minimalist faction signaling compared to traditional RTS games—no color-coded teams are explicitly documented. Faction identity relies on distinct unit model visual differences (Dark vs. Light unit aesthetics) rather than UI color overlays. The game communicates world-state primarily through territorial control visualization and unit availability/reinforcement patterns. Danger signals focus on enemy proximity (selection indicators) and unit health status rather than environmental hazards. The seventeenth year of war context creates implicit danger atmosphere without documented visual environmental degradation or destruction markers.
